1→# Kapow Data Import Tool — Design Document
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Kapow (College Power Search) has a fully defined PostgreSQL schema and a working
     6→API/frontend, but no data import pipeline. The database needs to be populated
     7→from government data (NCES College Scorecard, Clery Act crime stats), college
     8→ranking publications (US News, Forbes, Niche, etc.), athletic conference data
     9→(NCAA), climate data (Koppen), and other sources. This tool provides a single Go
    10→binary with subcommands to import all data sources, with download+cache support
    11→and proper documentation for sources that require web scraping.
    12→
    13→## Architecture
    14→
    15→### Module: `tools/kapow/` (standalone Go module, like `tools/samplesql/`)
    16→
    17→```
    18→tools/kapow/
    19→├── go.mod                       # github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/kapow
    20→├── main.go                      # CLI entrypoint: subcommand dispatch
    21→├── registry.go                  # Importer registration table + run-all orchestrator
    22→├── DATA_SOURCES.md              # Data source URLs, scraping instructions, update cadence
    23→│
    24→├── internal/
    25→│   ├── config/config.go         # DATABASE_URL, CACHE_DIR, LOG_LEVEL, DRY_RUN
    26→│   ├── dbutil/
    27→│   │   ├── connect.go           # pgxpool connection with retry
    28→│   │   ├── upsert.go            # UpsertInstitution, UpsertDataset, UpsertAnnualDataset, MergeJSONB
    29→│   │   ├── batch.go             # CopyFrom bulk insert, BatchExec
    30→│   │   └── views.go             # RefreshMaterializedViews
    31→│   ├── fetch/
    32→│   │   ├── fetch.go             # HTTP download + ETag cache + decompression + local fallback
    33→│   │   └── fetch_test.go
    34→│   ├── csvutil/
    35→│   │   ├── reader.go            # Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader with typed column access
    36→│   │   └── reader_test.go
    37→│   ├── namematch/
    38→│   │   ├── matcher.go           # Multi-tier institution name matching
    39→│   │   ├── normalize.go         # Name canonicalization rules
    40→│   │   ├── aliases.go           # Hardcoded alias overrides (~1000 institution name mappings)
    41→│   │   ├── fuzzy.go             # Tier 2: Jaro-Winkler fuzzy matching (adrg/strutil)
    42→│   │   ├── llm.go               # Tier 3: LLM-assisted curation (Claude API, optional)
    43→│   │   └── matcher_test.go
    44→│   └── ranking/
    45→│       ├── builder.go           # Opinion type, RankingBuilder: accumulate → batch flush
    46→│       └── builder_test.go
    47→│
    48→├── nces/                        # NCES College Scorecard (institution foundation)
    49→│   ├── nces.go
    50→│   ├── fields.go                # CSV column → JSONB key mapping (200+ fields)
    51→│   └── nces_test.go
    52→├── ncesfos/                     # NCES Field of Study (CIP codes)
    53→├── rankings/                    # All 9+ ranking publications (one file each)
    54→│   ├── usnews.go, forbes.go, niche.go, timeshighered.go,
    55→│   │   qs.go, washmonthly.go, wsj.go, arwu.go, money.go
    56→│   └── rankings_test.go
    57→├── ncaa/                        # NCAA athletics
    58→│   ├── ncaa.go
    59→│   └── sports.go                # Sport→gender/category mapping
    60→├── weather/                     # Koppen climate zones
    61→├── crime/                       # Clery Act crime stats
    62→├── admissions/                  # Application platforms (Common App, Coalition, etc.)
    63→│   └── platforms.go             # Embedded platform membership lists
    64→├── misc/                        # Partisan lean, AAU, metro area
    65→├── zipcoords/                   # ZIP code coordinates
    66→└── normalizer/                  # institution_normalized_alias generator
    67→```
    68→
    69→### CLI Design
    70→
    71→Single binary, subcommand dispatch via stdlib `flag`:
    72→
    73→```
    74→kapow <command> [flags]
    75→
    76→Commands:
    77→  nces             Import NCES College Scorecard institutions
    78→  nces-fos         Import NCES Field of Study data
    79→  rankings         Import rankings (--publication=usnews|forbes|niche|...)
    80→  ncaa             Import NCAA athletics data
    81→  weather          Import Koppen climate classification
    82→  crime            Import Clery Act crime statistics
    83→  admissions       Import application platform membership
    84→  misc             Import misc data (partisan lean, AAU, metro area)
    85→  zipcoords        Import ZIP code coordinates
    86→  normalizer       Generate normalized institution aliases
    87→  run-all          Run all importers in dependency order
    88→  match-review     Review unmatched names from last run (fuzzy + LLM suggestions)
    89→  sources          Print data source documentation
    90→
    91→Global flags:
    92→  --database-url   PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)
    93→  --cache-dir      Download cache dir (default: ~/.cache/kapow)
    94→  --log-level      debug|info|warn|error (default: info)
    95→  --dry-run        Parse and validate without writing to DB
    96→
    97→Per-command flags:
    98→  --file PATH      Use local file instead of downloading
    99→  --year YYYY      Data year (where applicable)
   100→  rankings --publication SLUG [--category SLUG]
   101→  run-all --skip=weather,metro  |  --only=nces,rankings
   102→```
   103→
   104→### Importer Interface
   105→
   106→```go
   107→type Importer interface {
   108→    Name() string
   109→    Description() string
   110→    Dependencies() []string
   111→    RegisterFlags(fs *flag.FlagSet)
   112→    Run(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config, pool *pgxpool.Pool, fetcher *fetch.Fetcher) error
   113→}
   114→```
   115→
   116→### Shared Infrastructure
   117→
   118→**`internal/fetch`** — Download + cache. HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified
   119→conditional requests. Cache keyed by URL hash. Transparent .zip/.gz/.xz
   120→decompression. `--file` flag bypasses download. Stale cache preferred over
   121→failure when source is temporarily unavailable.
   122→
   123→**`internal/csvutil`** — Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader. `Row.String("COL")`,
   124→`Row.Int("COL")`, `Row.OptionalFloat("COL")` typed accessors. Avoids
   125→repetitive index-based column access.
   126→
   127→**`internal/namematch`** — Multi-tier institution name resolution:
   128→
   129→- **Tier 1 — Deterministic** (free, instant, ~95% of matches):
   130→  Loads all institutions + aliases from DB into memory maps. Exact name →
   131→  normalized name → alias lookup. Also supports OPE6/OPE8 ID lookups.
   132→  Normalization: lowercase, strip diacritics, remove "The ", strip punctuation,
   133→  collapse whitespace. Includes ~1000 hardcoded alias overrides for institutions
   134→  whose external names differ from their NCES canonical names.
   135→
   136→- **Tier 2 — Fuzzy string similarity** (free, fast, ~3-4% more):
   137→  Uses [adrg/strutil](https://github.com/adrg/strutil) for Jaro-Winkler
   138→  similarity. When Tier 1 fails, computes similarity against all institutions
   139→  (pre-filtered by state when available). Matches above threshold (0.92) are
   140→  flagged as candidates. Returned with confidence scores for human review.
   141→
   142→- **Tier 3 — LLM-assisted curation** (optional, pay-per-use, long tail):
   143→  For names that Tier 1+2 can't resolve. Batches unmatched names with context
   144→  (source publication, state, ranking position, top fuzzy candidates) and sends
   145→  to Claude API for suggested matches. Suggestions presented for human approval.
   146→
   147→  All approved matches (from Tier 2 or 3) are persisted as new aliases in
   148→  `search_base.institution_alias` for future deterministic matching.
   149→
   150→- **`match-review` subcommand**: Interactive review of unmatched names from the
   151→  last import run. Shows fuzzy candidates + LLM suggestions. `--approve` saves
   152→  selected matches as aliases.
   153→
   154→**`internal/ranking`** — `Opinion` type (rank/score/tier/range methods).
   155→`RankingBuilder` accumulates opinions and batch-flushes to `search_base.ranking`
   156→metadata + `search_base.annual_dataset` + `institution.rankings` JSONB. All 9
   157→ranking importers use this; their own code is just parse + match + derive opinion.
   158→
   159→**`internal/dbutil`** — Connection pool with retry. `UpsertInstitution` (core
   160→columns + JSONB merge). `MergeJSONB` (append to institution JSONB column without
   161→overwriting other importers' data). `UpsertDataset` / `UpsertAnnualDataset`.
   162→`RefreshMaterializedViews` (called once at end of run-all).
   163→
   164→### Pipeline Ordering (`run-all`)
   165→
   166→```
   167→Layer 0 (no deps):   nces, zipcoords
   168→Layer 1 (needs nces): nces-fos, rankings/*, ncaa, weather, crime,
   169→                       admissions, misc/*
   170→Layer 2 (needs all):  normalizer
   171→Final:                RefreshMaterializedViews
   172→```
   173→
   174→## Data Sources
   175→
   176→### Data Update Calendar
   177→
   178→Each source has an "expected" date (when it typically drops) and a "by" date
   179→(99.5% confidence it's out, without being a full year later).
   180→
   181→| Source | Expected Release | By Date | Notes |
   182→|--------|-----------------|---------|-------|
   183→| **College Scorecard** | Late April–May | Jun 30 | Apr 23 2025, May 6 2024. Oct update adds FSA data. |
   184→| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same as above | Jun 30 | Bundled with institution data release |
   185→| **US News** | Late Sep | Oct 15 | Sep 23 2025, Sep 2024. Always September. |
   186→| **Forbes** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 26 2025, Sep 7 2024. Late Aug–early Sep. |
   187→| **Niche** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 25 2025. Consistently late August. |
   188→| **Times Higher Ed** | Early–mid Oct | Nov 1 | Oct 9 2025, Oct 10 2024. Always October. |
   189→| **QS World** | Mid Jun | Jul 15 | Jun 19 2025, Jun 4 2024. Always June. |
   190→| **Washington Monthly** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 24 2025. Published with Sep/Oct issue. |
   191→| **WSJ** | Early Sep | Oct 1 | Sep 5 2024. Fall release. |
   192→| **ARWU/Shanghai** | Mid Aug | Sep 1 | Aug 15 2025. Consistently mid-August since 2003. |
   193→| **Money** | Late Jun | Jul 31 | Jun 25 2025. Summer release. |
   194→| **Crime/Clery** | Oct 1 deadline | Nov 15 | Institutions must publish by Oct 1; DOE data files appear weeks later. |
   195→| **NCAA Directory** | Aug (academic year) | Sep 30 | Updated for each academic year. |
   196→| **Partisan Lean** | Post-election | Mar (odd years) | FiveThirtyEight updates after each election cycle. |
   197→| **ZIP Coordinates** | Stable | N/A | GeoNames data; updated continuously but rarely changes. |
   198→| **Koppen Climate** | Stable | N/A | Climate classifications; updated every ~10 years. |
   199→
   200→**Suggested annual import schedule:**
   201→1. **May–Jun**: College Scorecard + FOS (foundation refresh), QS, Money
   202→2. **Aug–Sep**: ARWU, Forbes, Niche, Washington Monthly, NCAA, US News, WSJ
   203→3. **Oct–Nov**: Times Higher Ed, Crime/Clery
   204→4. **After each run**: normalizer, materialized view refresh
   205→
   206→### Programmatic Download (automatable)
   207→
   208→| Source | URL / API | Format |
   209→|--------|-----------|--------|
   210→| **College Scorecard** | `https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/` — bulk CSV download | CSV (ZIP) |
   211→| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same site, FieldOfStudyData files | CSV (ZIP) |
   212→| **Forbes Rankings** | `https://www.forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/position/true.json?limit=1000&fields=...` | JSON |
   213→| **Niche Rankings** | `https://www.niche.com/api/renaissance/results/?type=private&type=public&listURL=best-colleges&page={i}&searchType=college` (109 pages, needs User-Agent + Referer headers, 200s sleep between requests) | JSON |
   214→| **Crime Data** | `https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list` — downloadable XLS, convert to CSV via libreoffice | XLS→CSV |
   215→| **Partisan Lean** | FiveThirtyEight CSV (GitHub) | CSV |
   216→| **ZIP Coordinates** | Public domain CSV (GeoNames or similar) | CSV |
   217→| **Koppen Climate** | Public domain ASCII grid | Text |
   218→| **PayScale** | `https://www.payscale.com/college-roi/page/2` — extract from page props JSON | HTML→JSON |
   219→
   220→### Web Scraping Required (semi-automated with docs)
   221→
   222→| Source | Website | Method | Notes |
   223→|--------|---------|--------|-------|
   224→| **ARWU/Shanghai** | `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` | Scrape HTML table or download PDF | No API. PDF at `shanghairanking.com/_pub/com/arwu/{year}/...pdf`. Use goquery for HTML or manual PDF download. |
   225→| **NCAA Sports** | `web3.ncaa.org/directory/memberList` | Scrape or manual export | NCAA Directory has member listings by division. May need browser automation or manual CSV export. |
   226→| **Money Magazine** | `money.com/best-colleges/` | Scrape HTML table | Extract `tr[data-id]` rows, parse `.stars-score` and `.col-name` via goquery |
   227→| **Princeton Review** | princetonreview.com | Scrape HTML | Category-based lists (most liberal, most conservative, etc.) |
   228→| **Times Higher Ed** | `timeshighereducation.com/world-university-rankings/{year}` | JSON API behind the page, or scrape | May need browser dev tools to find the API endpoint |
   229→| **QS World** | QS website | Previously available as TSV/CSV | Check if still downloadable or needs scraping |
   230→| **Washington Monthly** | washingtonmonthly.com | JSON or scrape | Check current availability |
   231→| **WSJ Rankings** | WSJ website | JSON embedded in page | `{ colleges: [...] }` structure |
   232→
   233→### Pre-curated / Aggregated Sources
   234→
   235→| Source | Location | Notes |
   236→|--------|----------|-------|
   237→| **US News Historical** | [frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings](https://github.com/frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings) | CSV archive 1984–2024. Free for public use. |
   238→| **Multi-ranking aggregate** | [frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data](https://github.com/frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data) | SAT, acceptance rate, US News/THE/QS/CSRankings |
   239→| **AAU Membership** | Embedded list (~71 institutions) | Stable; update when new members added |
   240→| **Common App / Coalition / etc.** | Embedded lists | Update annually from organization websites |
   241→
   242→### Approach for Non-Downloadable Sources
   243→
   244→For sources that require scraping, the importer will:
   245→1. **Try automated download first** (known API/URL patterns)
   246→2. **Fall back to cached local file** if download fails
   247→3. **Document manual steps** in `DATA_SOURCES.md` with exact commands
   248→4. **Accept `--file` flag** so user can provide manually-obtained data
   249→
   250→For scrapers specifically:
   251→- Use `net/http` + `github.com/PuerkitoBio/goquery` for HTML table extraction
   252→- Use `chromedp` only if JavaScript rendering is required
   253→- Keep scraping code isolated in each importer package for easy updates
   254→
   255→## Key Files to Reference
   256→
   257→- `tools/samplesql/main.go` — CLI pattern, flag parsing, DB connection
   258→- `tools/samplesql/extractor.go` — pgx query patterns, column detection
   259→- `api/internal/db/db.go` — Connection with retry pattern
   260→- `api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql` — Complete target schema
   261→- `api/internal/search/registry.go` — JSONB paths the API queries (consumer contract)
   262→
   263→## Issue Breakdown
   264→
   265→### Epic: Data Import Tool (`tools/kapow`)
   266→
   267→---
   268→
   269→#### 1. Scaffold importer module
   270→- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P0
   271→- **Deps**: none
   272→- **Description**: Create `tools/kapow/` Go module with `go.mod`
   273→  (`github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/kapow`), `main.go` with subcommand
   274→  dispatch via stdlib `flag`, `internal/config/config.go` (DATABASE_URL,
   275→  CACHE_DIR, LOG_LEVEL, DRY_RUN flags + env fallback). Include `Importer`
   276→  interface definition in `registry.go`. Compile and run `kapow --help`.
   277→- **Done when**: `go build ./...` succeeds, `kapow --help` prints usage.

   396→  (`ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(lon,lat),4326)`). Uses CopyFrom for performance.

   398→- **Done when**: zip_coordinates table populated with ~42,000 rows.

   400→#### 13. Implement Forbes ranking importer
   401→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   402→- **Deps**: 7
   403→- **Description**: `rankings/forbes.go`. Downloads from Forbes API JSON endpoint.
   404→  Parses organization name + rank. Uses name matcher → RankingBuilder. Unit test.
   405→- **Done when**: Forbes rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.
   406→
   407→#### 14. Implement Niche ranking importer
   408→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   409→- **Deps**: 7, 3
   410→- **Description**: `rankings/niche.go`. Paginated API scrape (109 pages, needs
   411→  User-Agent + Referer headers, 200s sleep between requests). Parses JSON
   412→  entities with IPEDS IDs (direct match, no name matching needed). Feeds
   413→  RankingBuilder. Document scraping procedure in DATA_SOURCES.md.
   414→- **Done when**: Niche rankings imported; scraping documented.
   415→
   416→#### 15. Implement Times Higher Ed ranking importer
   417→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   418→- **Deps**: 7
   419→- **Description**: `rankings/timeshighered.go`. Parses JSON. Handles numeric
   420→  ranks and tier bands ("400-500"). Includes overall score. Filters "Reporter"
   421→  entries. Name matching via aliases. Unit test.
   422→- **Done when**: THE rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.
   423→
   424→#### 16. Implement QS World ranking importer
   425→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   426→- **Deps**: 7, 4
   427→- **Description**: `rankings/qs.go`. Parses TSV. Handles tied ranks (trailing
   428→  '='). Multiple years per file. Overall score included. Unit test.
   429→- **Done when**: QS rankings imported for multiple years.
   430→
   431→#### 17. Implement Washington Monthly ranking importer
   432→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   433→- **Deps**: 7
   434→- **Description**: `rankings/washmonthly.go`. Parses JSON. Category-based
   435→  (national universities, liberal arts). Uses unit_id from JSON when available,
   436→  name matching as fallback. Unit test.
   437→- **Done when**: WashMonthly rankings in JSONB by category.
   438→
   439→#### 18. Implement WSJ ranking importer
   440→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   441→- **Deps**: 7
   442→- **Description**: `rankings/wsj.go`. Parses JSON `{ colleges: [...] }` with
   443→  unitid + rank. Direct unit_id matching + name fallback. Unit test.
   444→- **Done when**: WSJ rankings in institution.rankings JSONB.
   445→
   446→#### 19. Implement ARWU/Shanghai ranking importer
   447→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   448→- **Deps**: 7, 3
   449→- **Description**: `rankings/arwu.go`. Scrapes ranking table from
   450→  `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` using goquery or downloads PDF.
   451→  Parses name + national rank + total score. Name matching. Document scraping
   452→  procedure. Also support local JSON file for pre-scraped data.
   453→- **Done when**: ARWU rankings imported; scraping documented in DATA_SOURCES.md.
   454→
   455→#### 20. Implement Money ranking importer
   456→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   457→- **Deps**: 7
   458→- **Description**: `rankings/money.go`. Embedded data for current year.
   459→  Also document HTML scraping procedure for future updates (goquery on
   460→  `money.com/best-colleges/`, extract `tr[data-id]`). Unit test.
   461→- **Done when**: Money rankings imported from embedded data.
   462→
   463→#### 21. Implement Crime/Clery Act importer
   464→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   465→- **Deps**: 6, 4
   466→- **Description**: `crime/` package. Parses multiple CSVs (on-campus crime,
   467→  non-campus crime, on-campus VAWA, non-campus VAWA — converted from XLS via
   468→  libreoffice). Deduplicates by OPE8 preferring Main Campus. Aggregates crime
   469→  categories (murder, rape, robbery, etc.) and VAWA categories (dating violence,
   470→  stalking). Writes `institution.safety_and_security` JSONB + SAFETY dataset.
   471→  Document XLS→CSV conversion step. Unit + integration tests.
   472→- **Done when**: safety_and_security JSONB populated with crime + VAWA counts.
   473→
   474→#### 22. Implement Admissions importer
   475→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P2
   476→- **Deps**: 5, 6
   477→- **Description**: `admissions/` package. `platforms.go`: embedded membership
   478→  lists for Common App (~1500), Coalition (~150), ApplyTexas, Apply SUNY, UC
   479→  system, CSU system. `admissions.go`: determines platform membership by
   480→  name/alias matching + prefix detection (UC/CSU). Writes
   481→  `institution.admissions` JSONB + ADMISSIONS dataset. Unit + integration tests.
   482→- **Done when**: admissions JSONB has application platform flags for matched institutions.
   483→
   484→#### 23. Implement Misc importers (partisan lean, AAU, metro)
   485→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
   486→- **Deps**: 6
   487→- **Description**: `misc/` package. Three sub-importers:
   488→  `partisan.go` — parses FiveThirtyEight CSV, PostGIS spatial join to
   489→  congressional districts. `aau.go` — embedded list of ~71 AAU members, name
   490→  matching. `metro.go` — CBSA/metro area lookup via PostGIS spatial query.
   491→  All write to `institution.misc` JSONB + CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT dataset.
   492→  Unit tests for parsing; integration test for PostGIS joins.
   493→- **Done when**: misc JSONB has partisan_lean, aau_member, metro_area fields.
   494→
   495→#### 24. Implement Name Normalizer
   496→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
   497→- **Deps**: 5, 6 (ideally all importers complete, but not a hard dep)
   498→- **Description**: `normalizer/` package. Reads all institutions from DB, runs
   499→  `namematch.Normalize(name)`, detects collisions, writes to
   500→  `location.institution_normalized_alias` (unit_id, normalized_alias, city,
   501→  state_code). Should run last in the pipeline. Unit + integration tests.
   502→- **Done when**: institution_normalized_alias table populated for all institutions.
   503→
   504→#### 25. Implement run-all pipeline
   505→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
   506→- **Deps**: all importers (6–24)
   507→- **Description**: `run-all` subcommand in `registry.go`. Executes importers in
   508→  dependency order (Layer 0: nces + zipcoords → Layer 1: all others → Layer 2:
   509→  normalizer → Final: RefreshMaterializedViews). Supports `--skip=weather,metro`
   510→  and `--only=nces,rankings` flags. Logs per-importer timing and summary stats.
   511→  Stops on first error (with `--continue-on-error` flag to override).
   512→- **Done when**: `kapow run-all` populates a fresh DB end-to-end.
   513→
   514→#### 26. Implement namematch Tier 2 (fuzzy matching)
   515→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P3
   516→- **Deps**: 5
   517→- **Description**: `internal/namematch/fuzzy.go`. Uses `adrg/strutil` for
   518→  Jaro-Winkler similarity. When Tier 1 fails, computes similarity against all
   519→  institutions (pre-filtered by state when available from source data). Returns
   520→  top candidates above threshold (0.92) with confidence scores. Logs unmatched
   521→  names + fuzzy candidates to a JSON report file for review.
   522→- **Done when**: Unit test shows fuzzy match for misspelled institution name; report file generated.
   523→
   524→#### 27. Implement match-review subcommand
   525→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P4
   526→- **Deps**: 26
   527→- **Description**: `match-review` subcommand. Loads unmatched name report from
   528→  last import run. Displays each unmatched name with top fuzzy candidates.
   529→  `--approve ID` persists a match as a new alias in `search_base.institution_alias`.
   530→  `--reject ID` marks as known non-match (skip in future runs). Tracks review
   531→  state in a local JSON file.
   532→- **Done when**: Can review unmatched names interactively and persist aliases.
   533→
   534→#### 28. Implement namematch Tier 3 (LLM-assisted)
   535→- **Type**: feature | **Priority**: P4
   536→- **Deps**: 27
   537→- **Description**: `internal/namematch/llm.go`. `--llm-assist` flag on
   538→  match-review. Batches unmatched names with context (source, state, ranking,
   539→  fuzzy candidates) and sends to Claude API. Presents LLM suggestions alongside
   540→  fuzzy candidates in match-review. Uses `anthropic-sdk-go`. All matches still
   541→  require human approval.
   542→- **Done when**: LLM suggestions appear in match-review for unresolved names.
   543→
   544→#### 29. Write DATA_SOURCES.md
   545→- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P3
   546→- **Deps**: 6 (enough context to write; update as importers are added)
   547→- **Description**: `tools/kapow/DATA_SOURCES.md`. Complete documentation of
   548→  every data source: URL, access method (download/API/scrape), file format,
   549→  update cadence with expected + by dates, manual steps where automation isn't
   550→  possible (XLS→CSV conversion, NCAA directory export, ARWU scraping commands).
   551→  Include the annual import schedule.
   552→- **Done when**: Every data source documented with actionable instructions.
   553→
   554→#### 30. Update Makefile and documentation
   555→- **Type**: task | **Priority**: P3
   556→- **Deps**: 1
   557→- **Description**: Add to root Makefile: `kapow-build` (compiles binary),
   558→  `kapow-test` (runs tests), `kapow-test-unit` (short tests). Update
   559→  `tools/CLAUDE.md` with kapow tool documentation. Update root `CLAUDE.md` if
   560→  needed (add kapow tool to "Common task recipes").
   561→- **Done when**: `make kapow-build` and `make kapow-test-unit` work from root.

1→# Data Model Specification
     2→
     3→Comprehensive reference for the Kapow database schema, table definitions,
     4→relationships, indexes, and data flow.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Database
     9→
    10→- **Engine**: PostgreSQL 15+ with PostGIS extension
    11→- **Driver**: `jackc/pgx/v5` + `pgxpool` (context-aware, async connection pooling)
    12→- **Migrations**: `pressly/goose/v3`, embedded in the API binary via `api/migrations/migrations.go`
    13→- **SRID**: 4326 (WGS 84) for all geography columns
    14→
    15→---
    16→
    17→## Schemas
    18→
    19→| Schema         | Purpose                                                              |
    20→|----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
    21→| `search_base`  | Core institution data, datasets, rankings, materialized views        |
    22→| `location`     | US states, institution aliases, ZIP code coordinates (PostGIS)       |
    23→| `subscription` | Teams, team members, subscriptions, purchases                        |
    24→| `ugc`          | User-generated content: saved institutions, saved searches, prefs    |
    25→| `internal`     | Admin event log and user feedback                                    |
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## Enums
    30→
    31→| Enum                                  | Values |
    32→|---------------------------------------|--------|
    33→| `search_base.theme`                   | `ACADEMICS`, `ADMISSIONS`, `ATHLETICS`, `CORE`, `COST`, `CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT`, `DEMOGRAPHICS`, `FIELDS_OF_STUDY`, `LOCATION`, `METADATA`, `OUTCOMES`, `PHYSICAL_ENVIRONMENT`, `RANKING`, `REPUTATION`, `SAFETY`, `STUDENT_LIFE`, `WEATHER`, `OTHER` |
    34→| `search_base.credential_level`        | `CERTIFICATE`, `ASSOCIATE`, `BACHELOR`, `MASTER`, `DOCTORATE`, `PROFESSIONAL`, `OTHER` |
    35→| `subscription.role`                   | `OWNER`, `MEMBER`, `OBSERVER` |
    36→| `subscription.subscription_status`    | `ACTIVE`, `CANCELLED`, `EXPIRED`, `PAST_DUE`, `PENDING`, `TRIALING`, `UNPAID` |
    37→| `subscription.purchase_status`        | `CREATED`, `PENDING`, `FAILED`, `CANCELLED`, `ERROR`, `EXPIRED`, `SUCCEEDED`, `UNKNOWN` |
    38→| `ugc.intent`                          | `CERTAINLY`, `VERY_LIKELY`, `LIKELY`, `MAYBE`, `UNLIKELY`, `VERY_UNLIKELY`, `CERTAINLY_NOT` |

   405→## Materialized Views
   406→
   407→### `search_base.dataset_merged`
   408→
   409→**Primary search target.** Denormalized join of `institution` + up to 5 years
   410→of `annual_dataset` + all `dataset` rows, grouped per institution.
   411→
   412→**Construction logic** (migration v2):
   413→
   414→1. Select `annual_dataset` rows where `year > current_year - 5`, ordered by year.
   415→2. Aggregate per `(unit_id, theme, datakey)`: merge up to 5 yearly JSONB objects
   416→   via concatenation (later years override earlier keys).
   417→3. UNION with non-annual `dataset` rows.
   418→4. JOIN with `institution` and `location.state` for full name/location data.
   419→5. Aggregate all datakeys into a single `data` JSONB object per institution via
   420→   `jsonb_object_agg(datakey, data)`.
   421→
   422→**Computed columns**:
   423→
   424→| Column                          | Type       | Source                                         |
   425→|---------------------------------|------------|------------------------------------------------|
   426→| `unit_id`                       | `VARCHAR`  | `institution.unit_id`                          |
   427→| `ope6_id`                       | `VARCHAR`  | `institution.ope6_id`                          |
   428→| `ope8_id`                       | `VARCHAR`  | `institution.ope8_id`                          |
   429→| `institution_name`              | `VARCHAR`  | `institution.institution_name`                 |
   430→| `city`                          | `VARCHAR`  | `institution.city`                             |
   431→| `state`                         | `VARCHAR`  | `institution.state`                            |
   432→| `coordinates`                   | `geography`| `institution.coordinates`                      |
   433→| `longitude`                     | `float`    | `ST_X(coordinates::geometry)`                  |
   434→| `latitude`                      | `float`    | `ST_Y(coordinates::geometry)`                  |
   435→| `name_search_vector`            | `tsvector` | `to_tsvector('english', institution_name)`     |
   436→| `name_location_search_vector`   | `tsvector` | `to_tsvector('english', name + city + state + state_full_name)` |
   437→| `data`                          | `JSONB`    | `jsonb_object_agg(datakey, data)` from combined datasets |
   438→
   439→**Created with**: `WITH NO DATA` -- must be explicitly refreshed after data loads.
   440→

   525→## Foreign Key Map
   526→
   527→All foreign keys use `ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE` unless noted otherwise.
   528→
   529→```
   530→location.state
   531→  <- search_base.institution(state)
   532→
   533→search_base.institution
   534→  <- search_base.dataset(unit_id)
   535→  <- search_base.annual_dataset(unit_id)
   536→  <- search_base.institution_alias(unit_id)
   537→  <- location.institution_normalized_alias(unit_id)
   538→  <- ugc.saved_institution(unit_id)
   539→
   540→subscription.team
   541→  <- subscription.team_member(team_id)
   542→  <- subscription.subscription_slot(for_team_id)
   543→  <- ugc.saved_institution(team_id)
   544→  <- ugc.saved_search(team_id)
   545→  <- ugc.preference(team_id)          [ON DELETE CASCADE]
   546→
   547→subscription.subscription
   548→  <- subscription.subscription_slot(subscription_id)
   549→```

   553→## Migration History
   554→
   555→| Version | File                                | Purpose                                              |
   556→|---------|-------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
   557→| 1       | `00001_initial_schema.sql`          | All schemas, enums, tables, indexes, foreign keys    |
   558→| 2       | `00002_dataset_merged_view.sql`     | `dataset_merged` materialized view + indexes         |
   559→| 3       | `00003_extended_views.sql`          | `dataset_latest` view; unique indexes on dataset/annual_dataset |
   560→| 4       | `00004_state_type_column.sql`       | Add `type` column to `location.state`; classify entries |
   561→| 5       | `00005_add_subset_column.sql`       | Add `subset` column to `dataset` and `annual_dataset` |
   562→| 6       | `00006_team_member_user_unique.sql` | Unique constraint on `team_member(user_id)`          |
   563→| 7       | `00007_preference_table.sql`        | `ugc.preference` table with FK to `team`             |
   564→
   565→---
   566→
   567→## Data Flow
   568→
   569→1. **Ingestion**: Raw data from IPEDS and other sources is loaded into
   570→   `institution` (JSONB columns), `dataset` (theme-scoped), and
   571→   `annual_dataset` (year-scoped) tables.
   572→
   573→2. **Materialization**: The `dataset_merged` materialized view denormalizes
   574→   institution + dataset + annual_dataset into a single searchable row per
   575→   institution. The view merges up to 5 years of annual data (later years
   576→   override earlier keys). Must be refreshed after any data load via
   577→   `REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW search_base.dataset_merged`.
   578→
   579→3. **Search**: The search engine (`api/internal/search`) queries
   580→   `dataset_merged` exclusively. Filters translate to parameterized SQL
   581→   against the `data` JSONB column, tsvector columns, geography column,
   582→   and top-level columns. GIN indexes on `data` and tsvector columns
   583→   provide fast filtering; SP-GiST on `coordinates` enables spatial queries.
   584→
   585→4. **User data**: Authenticated users are assigned a personal team
   586→   (via `EnsureUser` middleware). Teams own `saved_institution`,
   587→   `saved_search`, and `preference` records in the `ugc` schema.
   588→
   589→5. **Subscriptions**: The `subscription` schema tracks commercial
   590→   subscriptions, slots allocated to teams, and purchase transactions.
   591→
   592→---

   597→  field names to columns and JSONB paths in `dataset_merged`.
   598→- **GraphQL schema**: `api/graph/schema/*.graphql` defines the API surface
   599→  that queries this data model.
   600→- **Fixture data**: `common/src/main/resources/data/` contains sample data
   601→  matching this schema, used by `make test-fixture`.
   602→- **Migration source**: `api/migrations/*.sql` (embedded via `api/migrations/migrations.go`).

    46→		}
    47→
    48→		setupLogging(cfg)
    49→		if err := runImporter(imp, cfg); err != nil {
    50→			slog.Error("import failed", "importer", cmd, "error", err)
    51→			os.Exit(1)
    52→		}
    53→		return
    54→	}
    55→
    56→	// Built-in commands.
    57→	switch cmd {
    58→	case "run-all":
    59→		fs := flag.NewFlagSet("run-all", flag.ExitOnError)
    60→		cfg.RegisterFlags(fs)
    61→		skipFlag := fs.String("skip", "", "comma-separated importers to skip")
    62→		onlyFlag := fs.String("only", "", "comma-separated importers to run (exclusive)")
    63→		if err := fs.Parse(os.Args[2:]); err != nil {
    64→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "error: %v\n", err)
    65→			os.Exit(1)
    66→		}
    67→
    68→		setupLogging(cfg)
    69→
    70→		skip := parseCSV(*skipFlag)
    71→		only := parseCSV(*onlyFlag)
    72→
    73→		if err := runAll(cfg, skip, only); err != nil {
    74→			slog.Error("run-all failed", "error", err)
    75→			os.Exit(1)
    76→		}
    77→	default:
    78→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "unknown command: %s\n\n", cmd)
    79→		printUsage()
    80→		os.Exit(1)
    81→	}
    82→}
    83→
    84→func runImporter(imp Importer, cfg *config.Config) error {
    85→	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
    86→	defer cancel()
    87→
    88→	if cfg.DryRun {
    89→		slog.Info("dry-run mode enabled — no database writes")
    90→		fetcher := fetch.New(cfg.CacheDir)
    91→		return imp.Run(ctx, cfg, nil, fetcher)
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	if cfg.DatabaseURL == "" {
    95→		return fmt.Errorf("kapow: DATABASE_URL is required (set via --database-url or env)")
    96→	}
    97→
    98→	pool, err := dbutil.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)
    99→	if err != nil {
   100→		return err
   101→	}
   102→	defer pool.Close()
   103→
   104→	fetcher := fetch.New(cfg.CacheDir)
   105→	return imp.Run(ctx, cfg, pool, fetcher)
   106→}
   107→
   108→func runAll(cfg *config.Config, skip, only map[string]bool) error {
   109→	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
   110→	defer cancel()
   111→
   112→	if cfg.DatabaseURL == "" {
   113→		return fmt.Errorf("kapow: DATABASE_URL is required")
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	pool, err := dbutil.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)
   117→	if err != nil {
   118→		return err
   119→	}
   120→	defer pool.Close()
   121→
   122→	fetcher := fetch.New(cfg.CacheDir)
   123→
   124→	if err := RunAll(ctx, cfg, pool, fetcher, skip, only); err != nil {
   125→		return err
   126→	}
   127→
   128→	slog.Info("refreshing materialized views")
   129→	return dbutil.RefreshMaterializedViews(ctx, pool)
   130→}
   131→
   132→func setupLogging(cfg *config.Config) {
   133→	level := cfg.SlogLevel()
   134→	handler := slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: level})
   135→	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(handler))
   136→}
   137→
   138→func parseCSV(s string) map[string]bool {
   139→	m := make(map[string]bool)
   140→	if s == "" {
   141→		return m
   142→	}
   143→	for _, v := range strings.Split(s, ",") {
   144→		v = strings.TrimSpace(v)
   145→		if v != "" {
   146→			m[v] = true
   147→		}
   148→	}
   149→	return m
   150→}
   151→
   152→func printUsage() {
   153→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, `kapow — Kapow data pipeline tool
   154→
   155→Usage: kapow <command> [flags]
   156→
   157→Commands:
   158→  fetch <source>   Download and archive data from a source
   159→  fetch all        Download everything
   160→  import <source>  Import from archive into DB
   161→  import all       Import all in dependency order
   162→  run-all          Fetch all + import all in dependency order
   163→  sources          Print data source documentation
   164→
   165→Available sources:
   166→`)
   167→	for _, imp := range registry {
   168→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  %-16s %s\n", imp.Name(), imp.Description())
   169→	}
   170→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, `
   171→Global flags:
   172→  --database-url   PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)
   173→  --cache-dir      Download cache dir (default: ~/.cache/kapow)
   174→  --log-level      debug|info|warn|error (default: info)
   175→  --dry-run        Parse and validate without writing to DB
   176→`)
   177→}
   178→
   179→func printSources() {
   180→	fmt.Println("See DATA_SOURCES.md in this directory for complete data source documentation.")
   181→}

1→// Package fetch provides an HTTP fetcher with local archive caching for
     2→// downloading data source files used by the kapow import pipelines.
     3→package fetch
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"archive/zip"
     7→	"bytes"
     8→	"compress/gzip"
     9→	"context"
    10→	"encoding/json"
    11→	"fmt"
    12→	"io"
    13→	"log/slog"
    14→	"net/http"
    15→	"os"
    16→	"os/exec"
    17→	"path/filepath"
    18→	"sort"
    19→	"strings"
    20→	"time"
    21→)
    22→
    23→// FetchConfig describes how to retrieve a data source file.
    24→type FetchConfig struct {
    25→	// Name is a short identifier (e.g. "nces") used in archive filenames.
    26→	Name string
    27→	// URL is the HTTP URL to download from.
    28→	URL string
    29→	// Format is the source format before re-compression: "csv", "csv.gz", "gz", "zip".
    30→	Format string
    31→}
    32→
    33→// cacheEntry is persisted as a JSON sidecar alongside each archive file.
    34→// It stores the HTTP cache validators from the last successful download so
    35→// that subsequent requests can use conditional GET (ETag / Last-Modified).
    36→type cacheEntry struct {
    37→	ETag         string `json:"etag,omitempty"`
    38→	LastModified string `json:"last_modified,omitempty"`
    39→	File         string `json:"file"` // absolute path to the .xz archive
    40→}
    41→
    42→// Fetcher downloads and caches source data files. It stores downloaded files
    43→// as xz-compressed archives in a local cache directory to avoid redundant
    44→// downloads across runs.
    45→type Fetcher struct {
    46→	cacheDir   string
    47→	httpClient *http.Client
    48→}
    49→
    50→// New creates a Fetcher that stores cached downloads in the given directory.
    51→func New(cacheDir string) *Fetcher {
    52→	return &Fetcher{
    53→		cacheDir:   cacheDir,
    54→		httpClient: &http.Client{},
    55→	}
    56→}
    57→
    58→// CacheDir returns the directory used for cached downloads.
    59→func (f *Fetcher) CacheDir() string {
    60→	return f.cacheDir
    61→}
    62→
    63→// Fetch retrieves the data source described by cfg, returning a reader over
    64→// the raw (decompressed) content. The caller is responsible for closing the
    65→// returned reader.
    66→//
    67→// Fetch logic:
    68→//  1. If no sidecar exists, check for manually-dropped archive files and
    69→//     return the newest one without hitting the network.
    70→//  2. Perform a conditional HTTP GET using ETag / Last-Modified from the
    71→//     sidecar when available.
    72→//  3. On 304 Not Modified, return the existing cached archive.
    73→//  4. On network error, fall back to the stale cached archive (if present)
    74→//     with a warning log.
    75→//  5. On 200 OK, decompress the source body, re-compress with xz -9e, save
    76→//     the archive and sidecar, and return the decompressed content.
    77→func (f *Fetcher) Fetch(ctx context.Context, cfg FetchConfig) (io.ReadCloser, error) {
    78→	if err := os.MkdirAll(f.cacheDir, 0o755); err != nil {
    79→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("fetch: create cache dir: %w", err)

## Data Sources, Schedules, Formats, and Pipeline Integration Summary

Based on my analysis of the Kapow data import plan (`kapow-o0j-data-import-tool.md`) and related documentation, here is the comprehensive extraction of all data source information:

---

### 1. DATA SOURCES OVERVIEW

The Kapow data import pipeline (`tools/kapow`) is designed to populate a PostgreSQL database with institutional data from 12+ sources, organized into themes that map directly to the database schema.

**Current Implementation Status:**
- Infrastructure complete: Config, DB utilities, fetch/cache, CSV parsing, institution name matching (Tier 1 + 2 fuzzy), and ranking builder
- No specific importer implementations yet (no nces/, rankings/, ncaa/, etc. packages)
- Registry system ready; importers defined by interface

---

### 2. DATA UPDATE CALENDAR

All sources have "expected release" and "by" dates (99.5% confidence it's available):

| Source | Expected Release | By Date | Notes |
|--------|-----------------|---------|-------|
| **College Scorecard** | Late April–May | Jun 30 | Apr 23 2025, May 6 2024. Oct update adds FSA data. |
| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same as above | Jun 30 | Bundled with institution data release |
| **US News** | Late Sep | Oct 15 | Sep 23 2025, Sep 2024. Always September. |
| **Forbes** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 26 2025, Sep 7 2024. Late Aug–early Sep. |
| **Niche** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 25 2025. Consistently late August. |
| **Times Higher Ed** | Early–mid Oct | Nov 1 | Oct 9 2025, Oct 10 2024. Always October. |
| **QS World** | Mid Jun | Jul 15 | Jun 19 2025, Jun 4 2024. Always June. |
| **Washington Monthly** | Late Aug | Sep 15 | Aug 24 2025. Published with Sep/Oct issue. |
| **WSJ** | Early Sep | Oct 1 | Sep 5 2024. Fall release. |
| **ARWU/Shanghai** | Mid Aug | Sep 1 | Aug 15 2025. Consistently mid-August since 2003. |
| **Money** | Late Jun | Jul 31 | Jun 25 2025. Summer release. |
| **Crime/Clery** | Oct 1 deadline | Nov 15 | Institutions must publish by Oct 1; DOE data files appear weeks later. |
| **NCAA Directory** | Aug (academic year) | Sep 30 | Updated for each academic year. |
| **Partisan Lean** | Post-election | Mar (odd years) | FiveThirtyEight updates after each election cycle. |
| **ZIP Coordinates** | Stable | N/A | GeoNames data; updated continuously but rarely changes. |
| **Koppen Climate** | Stable | N/A | Climate classifications; updated every ~10 years. |

**Suggested Annual Import Schedule:**
1. **May–Jun**: College Scorecard + FOS (foundation refresh), QS, Money
2. **Aug–Sep**: ARWU, Forbes, Niche, Washington Monthly, NCAA, US News, WSJ
3. **Oct–Nov**: Times Higher Ed, Crime/Clery
4. **After each run**: normalizer, materialized view refresh

---

### 3. DATA SOURCES WITH PROGRAMMATIC DOWNLOAD (Automatable)

These sources have public APIs or direct download URLs:

| Source | URL / API | Format |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| **College Scorecard** | `https://collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/` — bulk CSV download | CSV (ZIP) |
| **College Scorecard FOS** | Same site, FieldOfStudyData files | CSV (ZIP) |
| **Forbes Rankings** | `https://www.forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/position/true.json?limit=1000&fields=...` | JSON |
| **Niche Rankings** | `https://www.niche.com/api/renaissance/results/?type=private&type=public&listURL=best-colleges&page={i}&searchType=college` (109 pages, needs User-Agent + Referer headers, 200ms sleep between requests) | JSON |
| **Crime Data** | `https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list` — downloadable XLS, convert to CSV via libreoffice | XLS→CSV |
| **Partisan Lean** | FiveThirtyEight CSV (GitHub) | CSV |
| **ZIP Coordinates** | Public domain CSV (GeoNames or similar) | CSV |
| **Koppen Climate** | Public domain ASCII grid | Text |
| **PayScale** | `https://www.payscale.com/college-roi/page/2` — extract from page props JSON | HTML→JSON |

---

### 4. DATA SOURCES REQUIRING WEB SCRAPING (Semi-Automated with Docs)

These sources need manual steps or browser automation:

| Source | Website | Method | Notes |
|--------|---------|--------|-------|
| **ARWU/Shanghai** | `shanghairanking.com/rankings/arwu/{year}` | Scrape HTML table or download PDF | No API. PDF at `shanghairanking.com/_pub/com/arwu/{year}/...pdf`. Use goquery for HTML or manual PDF download. |
| **NCAA Sports** | `web3.ncaa.org/directory/memberList` | Scrape or manual export | NCAA Directory has member listings by division. May need browser automation or manual CSV export. |
| **Money Magazine** | `money.com/best-colleges/` | Scrape HTML table | Extract `tr[data-id]` rows, parse `.stars-score` and `.col-name` via goquery |
| **Princeton Review** | princetonreview.com | Scrape HTML | Category-based lists (most liberal, most conservative, etc.) |
| **Times Higher Ed** | `timeshighereducation.com/world-university-rankings/{year}` | JSON API behind the page, or scrape | May need browser dev tools to find the API endpoint |
| **QS World** | QS website | Previously available as TSV/CSV | Check if still downloadable or needs scraping |
| **Washington Monthly** | washingtonmonthly.com | JSON or scrape | Check current availability |
| **WSJ Rankings** | WSJ website | JSON embedded in page | `{ colleges: [...] }` structure |

---

### 5. PRE-CURATED / AGGREGATED SOURCES

Data already collected and published by others:

| Source | Location | Notes |
|--------|----------|-------|
| **US News Historical** | [frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings](https://github.com/frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings) | CSV archive 1984–2024. Free for public use. |
| **Multi-ranking aggregate** | [frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data](https://github.com/frishberg/US-College-Ranking-Data) | SAT, acceptance rate, US News/THE/QS/CSRankings |
| **AAU Membership** | Embedded list (~71 institutions) | Stable; update when new members added |
| **Common App / Coalition / etc.** | Embedded lists | Update annually from organization websites |

---

### 6. DATABASE SCHEMA INTEGRATION

Data maps to these tables/JSONB columns in the Kapow database:

**Institution Core:**
- `search_base.institution` (unit_id, ope6_id, ope8_id, institution_name, city, state, coordinates)
- JSONB columns: `nces`, `weather`, `rankings`, `athletics`, `admissions`, `fields_of_study`, `misc`, `safety_and_security`

**Related Tables:**
- `search_base.dataset` / `search_base.annual_dataset` — theme-scoped data with JSONB payloads
- `search_base.ranking` — ranking publication/category lookup table
- `search_base.cip_data` — Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP codes)
- `search_base.conference` — NCAA athletic conferences
- `location.institution_alias` — alternative institution names
- `location.institution_normalized_alias` — canonicalized aliases for search
- `location.zip_coordinates` — PostGIS geography for ZIP codes
- `location.state` — US states and territories

**Materialized Views (Search Target):**
- `search_base.dataset_merged` — denormalized institution + dataset + annual_dataset (5 years)
- `search_base.dataset_latest` — latest year only

---

### 7. INFRASTRUCTURE & SHARED UTILITIES

The framework provides reusable components:

**`internal/fetch`** — Download + cache
- HTTP GET with ETag/Last-Modified conditional requests
- Cache keyed by URL hash, stored as xz-compressed archives in `~/.cache/kapow`
- Transparent decompression (.zip, .gz, .xz)
- `--file` flag bypasses download for local files
- Stale cache preferred over failure when source unavailable

**`internal/csvutil`** — Header-mapped CSV/TSV reader
- `Row.String("COL")`, `Row.Int("COL")`, `Row.OptionalFloat("COL")` typed accessors
- Avoids repetitive index-based column access
- Supports missing columns gracefully

**`internal/namematch`** — Multi-tier institution name resolution
- **Tier 1** (deterministic, free, instant, ~95%): exact + normalized name + alias lookups + OPE6/OPE8 direct match
- **Tier 2** (fuzzy, free, fast, ~3-4% more): Jaro-Winkler similarity via `adrg/strutil`; pre-filtered by state
- **Tier 3** (LLM-assisted, optional, pay-per-use): Claude API for unmatched names with context
- Normalization: lowercase, strip diacritics, remove "The ", strip punctuation, collapse whitespace
- ~1000 hardcoded alias overrides in `aliases.go`

**`internal/ranking`** — Opinion accumulation and batch flush
- `Opinion` type: publication_slug, category_slug, method (rank/score/tier/range), year, unit_id, rank, score, tier
- `RankingBuilder`: accumulates opinions, `Flush(ctx)` writes to:
  - `search_base.ranking` (metadata)
  - `search_base.annual_dataset` (theme=RANKING)
  - `institution.rankings` JSONB (merge without overwrite)

**`internal/dbutil`** — Database utilities
- Connection pool with retry (reuses `api/internal/db` pattern)
- `UpsertInstitution(ctx, pool, inst)` — core columns + JSONB merge via `COALESCE || $N`
- `UpsertDataset`, `UpsertAnnualDataset` — insert or update theme-scoped data
- `MergeJSONB(ctx, pool, unit_id, col, data)` — append to JSONB without overwrite
- `RefreshMaterializedViews(ctx, pool)` — refresh `dataset_merged` and `dataset_latest`
- `CopyFrom` bulk insert wrapper for performance

**`internal/config`** — Configuration
- `DATABASE_URL`, `CACHE_DIR`, `LOG_LEVEL`, `DRY_RUN` flags + env fallback

---

### 8. CLI DESIGN

Single binary with subcommand dispatch:

```
kapow <command> [flags]

Commands:
  nces             Import NCES College Scorecard institutions
  nces-fos         Import NCES Field of Study data
  rankings         Import rankings (--publication=usnews|forbes|niche|...)
  ncaa             Import NCAA athletics data
  weather          Import Koppen climate classification
  crime            Import Clery Act crime statistics
  admissions       Import application platform membership
  misc             Import misc data (partisan lean, AAU, metro area)
  zipcoords        Import ZIP code coordinates
  normalizer       Generate normalized institution aliases
  run-all          Run all importers in dependency order
  match-review     Review unmatched names from last run (fuzzy + LLM suggestions)
  sources          Print data source documentation

Global flags:
  --database-url   PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)
  --cache-dir      Download cache dir (default: ~/.cache/kapow)
  --log-level      debug|info|warn|error (default: info)
  --dry-run        Parse and validate without writing to DB

Per-command flags:
  --file PATH      Use local file instead of downloading
  --year YYYY      Data year (where applicable)
  rankings --publication SLUG [--category SLUG]
  run-all --skip=weather,metro  |  --only=nces,rankings
```

---

### 9. IMPORTER INTERFACE

All importers implement this interface:

```go
type Importer interface {
    Name() string
    Description() string
    Dependencies() []string
    RegisterFlags(fs *flag.FlagSet)
    Run(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config, pool *pgxpool.Pool, fetcher *fetch.Fetcher) error
}
```

---

### 10. PIPELINE ORDERING (Dependency DAG)

The `run-all` orchestrator executes importers in dependency order using Kahn's topological sort:

```
Layer 0 (no deps):   nces, zipcoords
Layer 1 (needs nces): nces-fos, rankings/*, ncaa, weather, crime,
                       admissions, misc/*
Layer 2 (needs all):  normalizer
Final:                RefreshMaterializedViews
```

---

### 11. RELATED DOCUMENTATION & FILES

**Key references in the codebase:**
- `tools/kapow/DATA_SOURCES.md` — Complete data source documentation (planned)
- `api/migrations/00001_initial_schema.sql` — Target database schema
- `api/internal/search/registry.go` — JSONB paths the API queries (consumer contract)
